generatedSentence: Tonight, the handsome hacker, known only by his online alias, meticulously prepped his system. His ability for overclocking his browser for multimedia uploads was legendary, often pushing his hardware to a dangerous slowdown. He scoffed at the general public's careless data upkeep, especially those gawking at newsstand headlines without understanding the clandestine devilry behind the digital curtain. He stored his notes on a virtual nightstand, a mirror of his meticulously organized facility, often finding grace in the peachy glow of his monitor. His apartment, adorned with lavenders and afghans, was his sanctuary, a digital kitchenette where he crafted his next move, always looking for the nimble wavelength of opportunity. But a recent setback had left a hole in his usual infallibility. An arcade game's prototype, a dynamic network of vectors, was creating lockups across the city, generating an unacceptable rumble on the digital roadways. The owners, lordly and haughty, had hired a sentinel, a former soldier whose jurisdiction was rumored to impinge on the greyest areas of the law. This soldier was known for his swifter, corrective action, using a digital handcuff to knock down anyone who offends the system. The hacker felt a touchy anger rising; this was a challenge he couldn't outlive. He ducked a virtual kick from the soldier's avatar, a bizarre mix of an ichthus and monkfish, as they clashed in the digital backyard of the network. The soldier's gameplay was precise, full of evasive maneuvers, forcing the hacker to use every bit of his aftercare experience to avoid a total blowout. Suddenly, the soldier, with an almost vulgar display of force, cocked a digital arm, sending a powerful injection of malware, like a terrifying lightning strike, straight towards the hacker's systems. This nasty scenario was rapidly approaching critical mass, threatening a complete wreckage of his digital lifeline; he was truly baffled. With a sudden airdrop of a specially formulated ointment, the hacker deployed an alt-code that bejewels his defense, momentarily stunning the soldier's avatar, which surprisingly manifested as a womanish figure amongst flickering drum beats. He unleashed an upscaled playback of his most potent poem, a sequence of joyful, earthly code that pulsed with newfound power. The soldier, now merely a darky figure surrounded by chickens and worms, could only watch as the hacker, with a final general flourish of his keypads and knifes, repaired the system. His ability, once again proven, ensured a smooth future, no longer troubled by the phantom turkey of digital threats or the painful inhaler of defeat.

rawPanelSentences: Tonight, the handsome hacker, known only by his online alias, meticulously prepped his system. His ability for overclocking his browser for multimedia uploads was legendary, often pushing his hardware to a dangerous slowdown. He scoffed at the general public's careless data upkeep, especially those gawking at newsstand headlines without understanding the clandestine devilry behind the digital curtain. He stored his notes on a virtual nightstand, a mirror of his meticulously organized facility, often finding grace in the peachy glow of his monitor. His apartment, adorned with lavenders and afghans, was his sanctuary, a digital kitchenette where he crafted his next move, always looking for the nimble wavelength of opportunity.

But a recent setback had left a hole in his usual infallibility. An arcade game's prototype, a dynamic network of vectors, was creating lockups across the city, generating an unacceptable rumble on the digital roadways. The owners, lordly and haughty, had hired a sentinel, a former soldier whose jurisdiction was rumored to impinge on the greyest areas of the law. This soldier was known for his swifter, corrective action, using a digital handcuff to knock down anyone who offends the system. The hacker felt a touchy anger rising; this was a challenge he couldn't outlive.

He ducked a virtual kick from the soldier's avatar, a bizarre mix of an ichthus and monkfish, as they clashed in the digital backyard of the network. The soldier's gameplay was precise, full of evasive maneuvers, forcing the hacker to use every bit of his aftercare experience to avoid a total blowout. Suddenly, the soldier, with an almost vulgar display of force, cocked a digital arm, sending a powerful injection of malware, like a terrifying lightning strike, straight towards the hacker's systems. This nasty scenario was rapidly approaching critical mass, threatening a complete wreckage of his digital lifeline; he was truly baffled.

With a sudden airdrop of a specially formulated ointment, the hacker deployed an alt-code that bejewels his defense, momentarily stunning the soldier's avatar, which surprisingly manifested as a womanish figure amongst flickering drum beats. He unleashed an upscaled playback of his most potent poem, a sequence of joyful, earthly code that pulsed with newfound power. The soldier, now merely a darky figure surrounded by chickens and worms, could only watch as the hacker, with a final general flourish of his keypads and knifes, repaired the system. His ability, once again proven, ensured a smooth future, no longer troubled by the phantom turkey of digital threats or the painful inhaler of defeat.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, thick white gutters, clean black borders. A lone male hacker, wearing a futuristic data-suit with glowing circuitry and a sleek visor that obscures his eyes. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Hacker intently typing on a glowing holographic keyboard, multiple screens displaying complex code and multimedia streams around him, dramatic low-angle shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: A digital sentinel, armored with sharp, angular plating and an imposing stance, standing over a distorted cityscape, a high-angle shot emphasizing their dominance. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: The hacker's avatar, a stylized figure, dodging a dynamic energy attack from the sentinel's avatar within a surreal, glowing digital landscape, eye-level action shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The hacker, victorious, standing amidst a restored digital realm with calm, flowing data streams, a faint smile visible through his visor, wide shot showing the peaceful resolution, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting.

A bird in the genus Meleagris with a fan-shaped tail and wattled neck, especially the wild turkey (Meleagris gallopavo, now domesticated).
The flesh or meat of this bird eaten as food.
definitions.example: All week after Thanksgiving, I had turkey sandwiches for lunch.

definitions.definition: With a distinguishing word: a bird resembling the Meleagris gallopavo (for example, the bush turkey or brush turkey (Alectura lathami), and the water turkey (Anhinga anhinga)).
An act of throwing three strikes in a row.
A patient feigning symptoms; a person faking illness or injury; a malingerer.
A pack carried by a lumberman; a bindle; also, a large travel bag, a suitcase.
A failure.
definitions.example: That film was a turkey.

definitions.definition: (usually mildly) A foolish or inept person.
definitions.example: The turkey cut in front of me and then berated me for running into him.

definitions.definition: The tip of the external ear.
A small earpiece or earplug.
The part of an earbud that contours to the ear canal.
definitions.example: The shape of the nozzle prevents the use of aftermarket eartips.

definitions.definition: The removal of the tip of a cat's ear, done to indicate that the cat has been fixed.
definitions.example: a cat with an eartip

definitions.definition: The extreme end of something, especially when pointed; e.g. the sharp end of a pencil.
definitions.example: the tip of one's nose

definitions.definition: A piece of metal, fabric or other material used to cover the top of something for protection, utility or decoration.
definitions.example: a tip for an umbrella, a shoe, a gas burner, etc.

definitions.definition: The end of a bow of a stringed instrument that is not held.
(chiefly in the plural) A small piece of meat.
definitions.example: chicken tips over rice, pork tips, marinated alligator tips

definitions.definition: A piece of stiffened lining pasted on the inside of a hat crown.
A thin, boarded brush made of camel's hair, used by gilders in lifting gold leaf.
To provide with a tip; to cover the tip of.
(skittles) The knocking over of a skittle.
An act of tipping up or tilting.
An area or a place for dumping something, such as rubbish or refuse, as from a mine; a heap (see tipple); a dump.
Rubbish thrown from a quarry.
(by extension) A recycling centre.
A very untidy place.
The act of deflecting with one's fingers, especially the fingertips
A tram for expeditiously transferring coal.
(To cause) to become knocked over, fall down or overturn.
(To cause) to be, or come to be, in a tilted or sloping position; (to cause) to become unbalanced.
To drink.
To dump (refuse).
To pour a libation or a liquid from a container, particularly from a forty of malt liquor.
To deflect with one′s fingers, especially one′s fingertips.
A light blow or tap.
To hit quickly and lightly; to tap.
A gratuity; a small amount of money left for a bartender, waiter, taxi driver or other servant as a token of appreciation.
To give a small gratuity to, especially to an employee of someone who provides a service.
definitions.example: In some cities waiters must be tipped.

definitions.definition: To give, pass.
A piece of private or secret information, especially imparted by someone with expert knowledge about sporting odds, business performance etc.
A piece of advice.
To give a piece of private information to; to inform (someone) of a clue, secret knowledge, etc.
A kick or phase; one's current habits or behaviour.
A particular arena or sphere of interest; a front.
To increase in size, to scale up.
That has been scaled up.
To publicly disgrace, especially of a recreant knight.
To hoodwink or deceive (someone).
To bewilder completely; to confuse or perplex.
definitions.example: I am baffled by the contradictions and omissions in the instructions.

definitions.definition: To foil; to thwart.
To struggle in vain.
definitions.example: A ship baffles with the winds.

definitions.definition: Thoroughly confused, puzzled
Having baffles
To decorate or bedeck with jewels or gems.

In scientific usage, a multicellular organism that is usually mobile, whose cells are not encased in a rigid cell wall (distinguishing it from plants and fungi) and which derives energy solely from the consumption of other organisms (distinguishing it from plants).
definitions.example: A cat is an animal, not a plant. Humans are also animals, under the scientific definition, as we are not plants.

definitions.definition: In non-scientific usage, any member of the kingdom Animalia other than a human.
In non-scientific usage, any land-living vertebrate (i.e. not fishes, insects, etc.).
A person who behaves wildly; a bestial, brutal, brutish, cruel, or inhuman person.
definitions.example: My students are animals.

definitions.definition: A person of a particular type.
definitions.example: He's a political animal.

definitions.definition: Matter, thing.
definitions.example: a whole different animal

definitions.definition: To hit or knock (something), intentionally or accidentally, so that it falls.
definitions.example: As I took the can off the shelf, I knocked down the one beside it.

definitions.definition: To demolish.
definitions.example: We knocked down the garden shed when we moved.

definitions.definition: At an auction, to declare (something) sold with a blow from the gavel.
definitions.example: The picture was knocked down for £50.

definitions.definition: To reduce the price of.
definitions.example: They knocked it down by another £5, so we bought it.

definitions.definition: To drink fast.
definitions.example: I love to go down the pub and knock down pints of lager.

definitions.definition: (usually passive) To disassemble for shipment.
definitions.example: The furniture is shipped knocked down, so assembly is required.

definitions.definition: Conveying in demeanour the assumption of superiority; disdainful, supercilious.
One ring of a locking fetter for the hand or one pair.
To apply handcuffs to
To restrain or restrict.
definitions.example: Dang, I’m handcuffed by these regulations. I’d like to help but it’d be illegal.

definitions.definition: To make a physical impact on.
definitions.example: Loud noise can impinge on the eardrum, causing temporary hearing damage.

definitions.definition: To interfere with.
To have an effect upon, especially a negative one.
The power, right, or authority to interpret and apply the law.
The power or right to exercise authority.
The power or right to perform some action as part of applying the law.
The authority of a sovereign power to govern or legislate.
The limits or territory within which authority may be exercised.
Tending to avoid speaking openly or making revelations about oneself.
Directed towards avoidance or escape; evasive action.
A sentry, watch, or guard.
A private soldier.
A unique string of characters recognised by a computer program for processing in a special way; a keyword.
definitions.example: The  tag is a sentinel that suspends web-page processing and displays the subsequent text literally.

definitions.definition: A sentinel crab.
A sign of a health risk (e.g. a disease, an adverse effect).
definitions.example: sentinel animals can be used to explore endemic diseases.

definitions.definition: To watch over as a guard.
definitions.example: He sentineled the north wall.

definitions.definition: To post as guard.
definitions.example: He sentineled him on the north wall.

definitions.definition: To post a guard for.
definitions.example: He sentineled the north wall with just one man.

definitions.definition: A member of an army, of any rank.
A private in military service, as distinguished from an officer.
A guardsman.
A member of the Salvation Army.
A piece of buttered bread (or toast), cut into a long thin strip for dipping into a soft-boiled egg.
A term of affection for a young boy.
Someone who fights or toils well.
The red or cuckoo gurnard (Chelidonichthys cuculus).
One of the asexual polymorphic forms of termites, in which the head and jaws are very large and strong. The soldiers serve to defend the nest.
A red herring (cured kipper with flesh turned red).
To continue steadfast; to keep striving.
To serve as a soldier.
To intentionally restrict labor productivity; to work at the slowest rate that goes unpunished.
To take a ride on (another person's horse) without permission.
The act of correcting.
A substitution for an error or mistake.
Punishment that is intended to rehabilitate an offender.
An amount or quantity of something added or subtracted so as to correct.
A decline in a stock market price after a large rise.
(procedure word, military) a station's indication that previous information was incorrect and will continue with correct information from the last correct transmitted
definitions.example: I have four T-80 tanks at grid Three-niner-niner-four-eight-eight, Correction: Grid Three niner-niner-four-eight-five. How copy? Over.

definitions.definition: A domestic fowl, Gallus gallus, especially when young.
The meat from this bird eaten as food.
The young of any bird; a chick.
A coward.
A young or inexperienced person.
A young, attractive, slim man, usually having little body hair; compare chickenhawk.
The game of dare.
A simple dance in which the movements of a chicken are imitated.
To avoid a situation one is afraid of.
Good, smart; useful or beneficial.
definitions.example: a nifty trick for reducing your household energy bill

definitions.definition: Stylish.
To become obsessed about sex.
A woman with an excessive libido.
definitions.example: She's so horny she must be a nymphomaniac.

definitions.definition: Something nasty.
definitions.example: Processed foods are full of aspartame and other nasties.

definitions.definition: (preceded by "the") Sexual intercourse.
A video nasty.
Dirty, filthy.
Contemptible, unpleasant (of a person).
Objectionable, unpleasant (of a thing); repellent, offensive.
Indecent or offensive; obscene, lewd.
Spiteful, unkind.
Awkward, difficult to navigate; dangerous.
Grave or dangerous (of an accident, illness etc.).
Formidable, terrific; wicked.
A small table or cabinet, typically with drawers, placed at the head side of a bed.
definitions.example: He always kept a full glass of water on the nightstand.

definitions.definition: A characteristic or manner of an interaction; a behavior.
definitions.example: Watch the dynamic between the husband and wife when they disagree.

definitions.definition: A moving force.
definitions.example: The study of fluid dynamics quantifies turbulent and laminar flows.

definitions.definition: The varying loudness or volume of a song or the markings that indicate the loudness.
definitions.example: If you pay attention to the dynamics as you play, it's a very moving piece.

definitions.definition: A symbol in a musical score that indicates the desired level of volume.
(grammar) A verb that indicates continued or progressive action on the part of the subject.
Changing; active; in motion.
definitions.example: The environment is dynamic, changing with the years and the seasons.

definitions.definition: Powerful; energetic.
definitions.example: He was a dynamic and engaging speaker.

definitions.definition: Able to change and adapt.
Having to do with the volume of sound.
definitions.example: The dynamic marking in bar 40 is forte.

definitions.definition: Happening at runtime instead of being predetermined at compile time.
definitions.example: dynamic IP addresses

definitions.definition: Pertaining to dynamics, the branch of mechanics concerned with the effects of forces on the motion of objects.
(grammar) Of a verb: not stative, but fientive; indicating continued or progressive action on the part of the subject.
A flash of light produced by short-duration, high-voltage discharge of electricity within a cloud, between clouds, or between a cloud and the earth.
definitions.example: Although we did not see the lightning, we did hear the thunder.

definitions.definition: A discharge of this kind.
definitions.example: That tree was hit by lightning.

definitions.definition: Anything that moves very fast.
The act of making bright, or the state of being made bright; enlightenment; brightening, as of the mental powers.
To produce lightning.
Extremely fast or sudden; moving (as if) at the speed of lightning.

The length of a single cycle of a wave, as measured by the distance between one peak or trough of a wave and the next; it is often designated in physics as λ, and corresponds to the velocity of the wave divided by its frequency.
To hurt the feelings of; to displease; to make angry; to insult.
definitions.example: Your accusations offend me deeply.

definitions.definition: To feel or become offended; to take insult.
definitions.example: Don't worry. I don't offend easily.

definitions.definition: To physically harm, pain.
definitions.example: Strong light offends the eye.

definitions.definition: To annoy, cause discomfort or resent.
definitions.example: Physically enjoyable frivolity can still offend the conscience

definitions.definition: To sin, transgress divine law or moral rules.
To transgress or violate a law or moral requirement.
To cause to stumble; to cause to sin or to fall.
One who owns something.
definitions.example: The police recovered the stolen car and returned it to its owner.

definitions.definition: The captain of a ship.
To live longer than; continue to live after the death of; overlive; survive.
To live through or past (a given time).
To surpass in duration; outlast.
To live longer; continue to live.
A blanket or throw, usually crocheted or knitted.
A type of iced chocolate biscuit, sometimes containing cornflakes and topped with a walnut.
Displaying or feeling anger.
definitions.example: An angry mob started looting the warehouse.

definitions.definition: (said about a wound or a rash) Inflamed and painful.
definitions.example: The broken glass left two angry cuts across my arm.

definitions.definition: (said about the elements, like the sky or the sea) Dark and stormy, menacing.
definitions.example: Angry clouds raced across the sky.

definitions.definition: In rhythm, the second half of a divided beat.
As a coordinating conjunction; expressing two elements to be taken together or in addition to each other.
(heading) Expressing a condition.
A small board with keys primarily used for tactile input into a machine.
definitions.example: Many laptop computers have special function keys which turn part of the alphabetical keyboard into a numeric keypad.

definitions.definition: To cut with a knife.
To use a knife to injure or kill by stabbing, slashing, or otherwise using the sharp edge of the knife as a weapon.
To cut through as if with a knife.
To betray, especially in the context of a political slate.
To positively ignore, especially in order to denigrate. compare cut
An original form or object which is a basis for other forms or objects (particularly manufactured items), or for its generalizations and models.
An early sample or model built to test a concept or process.
definitions.example: The prototype had loose wires and rough edges, but it worked.

definitions.definition: A declaration of a function that specifies the name, return type, and parameters, but none of the body or actual code.
An instance of a category or a concept that combines its most representative attributes.
definitions.example: A robin is a prototype of a bird; a penguin is not.

definitions.definition: A type of race car, a racing sports car not based on a production car. A 4-wheeled cockpit-seating car built especially for racing on sports car circuits, that does not use the silhouette related to a consumer road car.
To create a prototype of.
A literary piece written in verse.
A piece of writing in the tradition of poetry, an instance of poetry.
A piece of poetic writing, that is with an intensity or depth of expression or inspiration greater than is usual in prose.
A meeting or get-together; a party or social function.
definitions.example: I met her at a gathering of engineers and scientists.

definitions.definition: A group of people or things.
definitions.example: A gathering of fruit.

definitions.definition: A section, a group of bifolios, or sheets of paper, stacked together and folded in half.
definitions.example: This gathering machine forms the backbone of a bookbinding operation.

definitions.definition: A charitable contribution; a collection.
A tumor or boil suppurated or maturated; an abscess.
Feeling or causing joy.
(collective or in the plural) That which is of the earth or earthly; a terrestrial being.
A slightest chance (of success etc.) or idea (about something).
Relating to the earth or this world, as opposed to heaven; terrestrial.
definitions.example: earthly joys

definitions.definition: (negative) Used for emphasis
Made of earth; earthy.
In an earthly manner
Fast; quick; rapid.
Capable of moving at high speeds.
A spoken or written remark.
definitions.example: I have no comment on that.

definitions.definition: A remark embedded in source code in such a way that it will be ignored by the compiler or interpreter, typically to help people to understand the code.
To remark.
(with "on" or "about") To make remarks or notes.
To comment or remark on.
(of code) To insert comments into (source code).
definitions.example: I wish I'd commented this complicated algorithm back when I remembered how it worked.

definitions.definition: (of code) To comment out (code); to disable by converting into a comment.
To lift the cock of a firearm or crossbow; to prepare (a gun or crossbow) to be fired.
To be prepared to be triggered by having the cock lifted.
definitions.example: In the darkness, the gun cocked loudly.

definitions.definition: To erect; to turn up.
To copulate with.
To turn or twist something upwards or to one side; to lift or tilt (e.g. headwear) boastfully.
definitions.example: He cocked his hat jauntily.

definitions.definition: To turn (the eye) obliquely and partially close its lid, as an expression of derision or insinuation.
To strut; to swagger; to look big, pert, or menacing.
To make a nestle-cock of, to pamper or spoil (of children)
To form into piles.
Drunk
(in combination) Having a specified form or penis or a specific number of penises
definitions.example: The large-cocked man entered the dressing room.

definitions.definition: Something that is not acceptable.
Unsatisfactory; not acceptable
Not conforming to accepted usage
A way used as a road.
The main or central portion of a road, used by the vehicles.
The portion of a bridge or railway used by traffic.
A low, heavy, continuous sound, such as that of thunder or a hungry stomach.
definitions.example: The rumble from passing trucks made it hard to sleep at night.

definitions.definition: A street fight or brawl.
A rotating cask or box in which small articles are smoothed or polished by friction against each other.
A seat for servants, behind the body of a carriage.
To make a low, heavy, continuous sound.
definitions.example: I could hear the thunder rumbling in the distance.

definitions.definition: To discover deceitful or underhanded behaviour.
definitions.example: The police is going to rumble your hideout.

definitions.definition: To move while making a rumbling noise.
definitions.example: The truck rumbled over the rough road.

definitions.definition: To fight; to brawl.
(of a game controller) to provide haptic feedback by vibrating.
To cause to pass through a rumble, or polishing machine.
To murmur; to ripple.
An onomatopoeia describing a rumbling noise
A course over which any type of races are run.
A characteristic circular erosion pattern in deposition processes.
Of or relating to a lord.
definitions.example: Show us your lordly might: demonstrate that you can order people and get them to obey.

definitions.definition: Having the qualities of a lord; lordlike; noble
Appropriate for, or suitable to, a lord; glorious.
Proud; haughty; imperious; insolent.
In the manner of a lord. Showing command or nobility.
A generally tubular invertebrate of the annelid phylum; an earthworm.
More loosely, any of various tubular invertebrates resembling annelids but not closely related to them, such as velvet worms, acorn worms, flatworms, or roundworms.
A type of wingless "dragon", especially a gigantic sea serpent.
Either a mythical "dragon" (especially wingless), a gigantic sea serpent, or a creature that resembles a Mongolian death worm.
A contemptible or devious being.
definitions.example: Don't try to run away, you little worm!

definitions.definition: A self-replicating program that propagates through a network.
A graphical representation of the total runs scored in an innings.
Anything helical, especially the thread of a screw.
Any creeping or crawling animal, such as a snake, snail, or caterpillar.
An internal tormentor; something that gnaws or afflicts one’s mind with remorse.
A strip of linked tiles sharing parallel edges in a tiling.
The lytta.
(preceded by definite article) A dance, or dance move, in which the dancer lies on the floor and undulates the body horizontally thereby moving forwards.
To make (one's way) with a crawling motion.
definitions.example: We wormed our way through the underbrush.

definitions.definition: To move with one's body dragging the ground.
To work one's way by artful or devious means.
To work (one's way or oneself) (into) gradually or slowly; to insinuate.
definitions.example: He wormed his way into the organization

definitions.definition: To effect, remove, drive, draw, or the like, by slow and secret means; often followed by out.
(in “worm out of”) To drag out of, to get information that someone is reluctant or unwilling to give (through artful or devious means or by pleading or asking repeatedly).
To fill in the contlines of (a rope) before parcelling and serving.
definitions.example: Worm and parcel with the lay; turn and serve the other way.

definitions.definition: To deworm (an animal).
To cut the worm, or lytta, from under the tongue of (a dog, etc.) for the purpose of checking a disposition to gnaw, and formerly supposed to guard against canine madness.
To clean by means of a worm; to draw a wad or cartridge from, as a firearm.
High pitch, of a voice or instrument; especially, the octave above the top line of the treble stave.
A state of excitement, a heightened emotional condition.
A directed quantity, one with both magnitude and direction; the signed difference between two points.
An ordered tuple representing a directed quantity or the signed difference between two points.
Any member of a (generalized) vector space.
definitions.example: The vectors in {\mathbb Q}[X] are the single-variable polynomials with rational coefficients: one is \textstyle x^{42}+\frac1{137}x-1.

definitions.definition: A chosen course or direction for motion, as of an aircraft.
A carrier of a disease-causing agent.
(by extension) A person or entity that passes along an urban legend or other meme.
A recurring psychosocial issue that stimulates growth and development in the personality.
The way in which the eyes are drawn across the visual text. The trail that a book cover can encourage the eyes to follow from certain objects to others.

(operating systems) A memory address containing the address of a code entry point, usually one which is part of a table and often one that is dereferenced and jumped to during the execution of an interrupt.
A one-dimensional array.
A graphical representation using outlines; vector graphics.
definitions.example: a vector image

definitions.definition: A DNA molecule used to carry genetic information from one organism into another.
To set (particularly an aircraft) on a course toward a selected point.
To redirect to a vector, or code entry point.
The replaying of something previously recorded, especially sound or moving images.
